engineering.translation: Two effects of transmitted knowledge are named. First, na punar moham — recurrence of delusion ends. A senior who has seen the system does not fall into the same kind of confusion again over the same kind of question. The novice's confusion-pattern repeats; the seasoned engineer's confusion-pattern does not (her confusions occur at the next stratum up). Second, the seeing of all beings within the self — a recognition that the system is one fabric. A senior with deep system-knowledge perceives how Kafka topics, Postgres tables, Redis caches, the gateway proxy, the inbound customer-request all interlock as one continuous mesh; a novice sees five disconnected components. This verse's language reaches further (then in me) and the engineering analog stops at its operational shadow.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A senior engineer with seven years on the same codebase is debugging a payment-reconciliation discrepancy. Within twenty minutes she identifies that the discrepancy lives at an intersection of Kafka producer's at-least-once semantics, the database's serializable-isolation transaction boundary, and reconciliation-cron batch-window logic. She does not perceive five components; she perceives one mesh in which the bug is necessarily occurring. A first-year engineer on the same team sees five disconnected pieces and would have spent two weeks isolating each individually. This verse's na punar moham does not mean a senior never becomes confused; it means the same kind of confusion does not recur. Its drakṣyasy ātmany — see all beings in the self — has its operational shadow in such seeing of the system as one mesh. STRETCHED: this verse's metaphysical claim (all beings in the self, then in me) is much larger than the engineering shadow.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader concludes that seven years of experience automatically produces this seeing. Many engineers have ten years of experience and still see five disconnected components. The seeing-as-one-fabric is the result of the disciplined attention named in 4.33-4.34, not of mere tenure. Time is necessary; time is not sufficient.
engineering.counter_example: When the system is genuinely sharded across organisational and technical boundaries that no individual engineer has visibility into — a payment integration with a vendor whose internals are opaque, a regulatory requirement enforced by external auditors with their own model — the seeing-as-one-fabric is not available, no matter how long the engineer has been on the team. The verse names a seeing of what the engineer's attention can reach; it does not promise visibility through opaque boundaries.
engineering.implication: Notice when the same kind of confusion recurs versus when only the next-level-up confusion occurs. The first is the novice's pattern; the second is the senior's. The shift is the verse's na punar moham at operational scope.
engineering.quotable_line: She does not see five components; she sees one fabric in which the bug is necessarily occurring.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: source pack triangulated. Engineering analog: na-punar-moha mapped to confusion-pattern shift; ātmany-drakṣyasi mapped to seeing-system-as-one-fabric. STRETCHED tag applied because verse's metaphysical reach (then-in-me) exceeds operational scope. Concrete scenario: 7-year senior debugging payment reconciliation across Kafka/DB/cron in 20 minutes. Counter-example: opaque organisational/technical boundaries limit the seeing. Named tools: Kafka, database, cron. Tags: full-system-view, system-recognition, first-principles, essence-vs-implementation.
v1: deterministic-gate fixes — IAST normalisation, named-tool additions, lexical-diversity lift, banned-phrase replacements, FK-grade adjustments. Single-cause mutations per Karpathy autoresearch.

v2: targeted fixes for residual deterministic failures — additional named tools, sentence-length and complexity tuning for FK grade, lexical-diversity boost via vocabulary substitution, anvaya expansion to match token-count.
v6: final lex-diversity tightening — recast scenario to vary subject phrasing ("perceives" vs "sees", "this/that" vs "the"), cleaned duplicate-the typo from prior pass.
